ships.name: Buttle
ships.description: A diabolical conglomeration, the Buttle maintains the shipping and transport potential of the Shuttle while obtaining the weapons and speed of the Berserker, the two ships it's created from.

ships.name: Cleaver
ships.description: The Cleaver is essentially a more powerful version of the Sunder, made by a small company on Shroud and distributed to a few other select planets. The designers of ALR Manufacturing had great plans to show who the Cleaver belonged to, but the builders ended up deciding to use parts from the original Sunder, and the ship did not look nearly as colorful as the original concept dictated. As the shipyards use imported products such as the Tractor Beam, the Cleaver has a rather high price tag. 	Following the tradition, the Cleaver has a roomy cargo bay; enough to make anyone flying it rich. 	According to some, it also carries special tools that allow it to harvest resources from the surface of mineral-rich planets or moons.

ships.name: Cutlass
ships.description: This ship is a modified version of a captured Navy Gunboat. Although there is little most pirates can do to improve the already advanced shielding, they can still weld on a few extra hull reinforcements and an extra pair of guns. This gives them a tough and reasonably fast gun platform to quickly attack, plunder, and escape.

ships.name: Dragonfly
ships.description: The Dragonfly was a light transport, designed for bringing a small number of very rich passengers - Syndicate CEOs, for example - from one planet to another with speed and style. It makes up for its light armament by being able to outrun nearly every other ship in the sky. 	That is, it was a light transport until a scheming Megaparsec engineer came up with a combination of the Arrow and the Wasp, at which point it became something much deadlier. While still not strong enough for close combat, the Dragonfly can easily defeat much larger enemies by simply staying away and bombarding them with missiles.

ships.name: Hailmaker
ships.description: The Navy developed the Hailmaker as a replacement for the Rainmaker around the time that the Typhoon was produced. It uses much more powerful explosives, but its mobility has not increased.

ships.name: Marauder Boxwing
ships.description: After introducing the "Type F" cargo pod for the Hauler, it became apparent to merchant captains that the new carriers could offer more flexibility within their fleet if there were a cargo box they could attach to the fighter arms. Enter the "F6-C: Boxwing," the Fighter that doesn't fight, but carries cargo instead. Cargo preservation equipment gives the Boxwing much more mass and very poor heat dissipation characteristics compared to other fighters. That it cannot carry any weapons is only part of the reason jocks call it "Flying Coffin" amongst themselves; besides the ship resembling one and being very sluggish, the pilot "seat" doubles as a bunk, and is cramped beyond the comfort level of most. The interactive panoramic window H.U.D. is the only thing that keeps claustrophobic episodes from becoming a major problem. 	Well, the Boxwing wasn't supposed to fight. This maraudified version has a single gun port, equipped with a deadly weapon - the Quad Beam Laser. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

ships.name: Marauder Combat Drone
ships.description: Combat drones are pilotless attack ships used primarily by the Republic Navy. Although very weak and easy to destroy, they can be very effective in large numbers. Because drones do not need a cockpit, they can be filled entirely with equipment and solid metal, which makes their hulls stronger than other small ships. 	This Combat Drone has been captured from the Navy and Maraudified. It now has space for a Heavy Laser and has a thicker hull - though this comes at the cost of almost doubled mass and a slightly lower top speed. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

ships.name: Mining Barge
ships.description: The Mining Barge is an ancient creation, predating the Sunder by many hundreds of years. Only one is left in existence, and since it lacks a hyperdrive, it won't be going anywhere soon. 	The pirates of the Core Mining Concern use this ship in the Nashira system to extract valuable ores from asteroids.

ships.name: Recruiter
ships.description: When the Headhunter saw a huge success in sales, the feedback polls that Lionheart sent out only found one problem - the hull was too fragile. Their newer iteration, called the Recruiter, remedies the hull issues, although it had to lose a turret mount to do so, and the shield matrices are much less advanced.

ships.name: Rumskib Destroyer
ships.description: The Republic Destroyer is the newest Navy design, a smaller version of the Cruiser designed to fight toe-to-toe with even the largest pirates. Although it can reach higher top speeds than any of the other large Navy ships, it takes longer to get there - unless it uses its afterburner.

ships.name: Rumskib Marauder Behemoth
ships.description: The Behemoth is a recent ship design from Betelgeuse Shipyards, designed as an alternative to the Syndicate Bulk Freighter. Although its cargo capacity is somewhat lower, it is also more maneuverable, and packs a heavier armament by default. 	This Marauder version has been heavily modified and is slower with a greater hull strength.

ships.name: Tanager
ships.description: The Tanager is a new model of transport ship from Tarazed Shipyards. More nimble compared to models of shuttlecraft from Betelgeuse, it is also supplemented with a greater capacity for defensive armaments; a necessity in the region of space that surrounds Tarazed. 	The Tanager also comes with a decent amount of free space, allowing for a much greater level of customization.

ships.name: Hailstone
ships.description: The first in a new line of experimental technology, the Hailstone proudly stands as the first Wanderer fighter ever. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

ships.name: Squall
ships.description: The first carrier that the Wanderers have built for millennia, the Squall was made to push back Unfettered invasions and attack new enemies in old Korath space.

ships.name: Swan (Rumskib)
ships.description: Historically, the Remnant have not needed to support remote fleet operations. It had, however, been anticipated as a part of their contingency plans to support large scale evacuations. The first Remnant survived by running, modern Remnant prosper through mobility, and it is expected that the Remnant of the future shall thrive by those same means. 	The Swan, thus, was designed first and foremost to be a refueler. Not only carrying a prodigous amount of fuel as well as massive arrays to collect more, but also ferrying a number of fighters and drones that can be used to deliver fuel to other ships in the fleet. It can also carry a significant amount of cargo. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Swan's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship. The Remnant engineers note that, due to its size, the Swan's cloak is both slower and consumes significantly more energy and fuel than other present-day designs.

ships.name: Za'lek Sting
ships.description: The Za'lek Sting is a ship from the game Naev. Unfortunately, the 3D animation can't be ported to Endless Sky.

ships.name: Mining Drone (Rumskib Start)
ships.description: The semi-autonomous Mining Drone is used by the Syndicate alongside the Sunder mining vessel in a supportive role. Not being much more than a Mining Laser with an engine stuck to the rear of it, these drones do not last long in combat with other ships due to how fragile they are. 	Modifications on Luna made to this particular drone give it integrated steering and thrust, in addition to a built-in bunk. These changes have also required it to hold one crew member.

variants.name: Buttle (Derelict)
variants.description: A diabolical conglomeration, the Buttle maintains the shipping and transport potential of the Shuttle while obtaining the weapons and speed of the Berserker, the two ships it's created from.

variants.name: Cleaver (Derelict)
variants.description: The Cleaver is essentially a more powerful version of the Sunder, made by a small company on Shroud and distributed to a few other select planets. The designers of ALR Manufacturing had great plans to show who the Cleaver belonged to, but the builders ended up deciding to use parts from the original Sunder, and the ship did not look nearly as colorful as the original concept dictated. As the shipyards use imported products such as the Tractor Beam, the Cleaver has a rather high price tag. 	Following the tradition, the Cleaver has a roomy cargo bay; enough to make anyone flying it rich. 	According to some, it also carries special tools that allow it to harvest resources from the surface of mineral-rich planets or moons.

variants.name: Cutlass (Derelict)
variants.description: This ship is a modified version of a captured Navy Gunboat. Although there is little most pirates can do to improve the already advanced shielding, they can still weld on a few extra hull reinforcements and an extra pair of guns. This gives them a tough and reasonably fast gun platform to quickly attack, plunder, and escape.

variants.name: Dragonfly (Derelict)
variants.description: The Dragonfly was a light transport, designed for bringing a small number of very rich passengers - Syndicate CEOs, for example - from one planet to another with speed and style. It makes up for its light armament by being able to outrun nearly every other ship in the sky. 	That is, it was a light transport until a scheming Megaparsec engineer came up with a combination of the Arrow and the Wasp, at which point it became something much deadlier. While still not strong enough for close combat, the Dragonfly can easily defeat much larger enemies by simply staying away and bombarding them with missiles.

variants.description: When the Headhunter saw a huge success in sales, the feedback polls that Lionheart sent out only found one problem - the hull was too fragile. Their newer iteration, called the Recruiter, remedies the hull issues, although it had to lose a turret mount to do so, and the shield matrices are much less advanced.

variants.name: Rumskib Marauder Behemoth (Derelict)
variants.description: The Behemoth is a recent ship design from Betelgeuse Shipyards, designed as an alternative to the Syndicate Bulk Freighter. Although its cargo capacity is somewhat lower, it is also more maneuverable, and packs a heavier armament by default. 	This Marauder version has been heavily modified and is slower with a greater hull strength.

variants.name: Tanager (Derelict)
variants.description: The Tanager is a new model of transport ship from Tarazed Shipyards. More nimble compared to models of shuttlecraft from Betelgeuse, it is also supplemented with a greater capacity for defensive armaments; a necessity in the region of space that surrounds Tarazed. 	The Tanager also comes with a decent amount of free space, allowing for a much greater level of customization.

variants.name: Buttle (Northern)
variants.description: A diabolical conglomeration, the Buttle maintains the shipping and transport potential of the Shuttle while obtaining the weapons and speed of the Berserker, the two ships it's created from.

variants.name: Cleaver (Defense)
variants.description: The Cleaver is essentially a more powerful version of the Sunder, made by a small company on Shroud and distributed to a few other select planets. The designers of ALR Manufacturing had great plans to show who the Cleaver belonged to, but the builders ended up deciding to use parts from the original Sunder, and the ship did not look nearly as colorful as the original concept dictated. As the shipyards use imported products such as the Tractor Beam, the Cleaver has a rather high price tag. 	Following the tradition, the Cleaver has a roomy cargo bay; enough to make anyone flying it rich. 	According to some, it also carries special tools that allow it to harvest resources from the surface of mineral-rich planets or moons.

variants.name: Modified Argosy (Gat)
variants.description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

variants.name: Ladybug (New Description)
variants.description: These sensor-laden exploration vessels hail back to a more peaceful era of the Hai. Though they have no true ramscoop, a technology the Hai never developed, their built-in collection gills magnify the natural ramscoop present in all ships. These expensive features are the reason the ship has been replaced with the Lightning Bug. However, this ship has seen combat in recent years against human pirates. With room for plentiful shield regeneration, good maneuverability, and the range to strike deep in pirate territory, their long sleep has ended.

variants.name: Splinter (Miner)
variants.description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by the Megaparsec shipyard. It is designed to be faster and more maneuverable than other ships of a similar size, in order to serve as a heavy escort ship or as part of a planetary defense and patrol fleet. Because of its intended use in merchant convoys, it also has a fair amount of cargo space.

variants.name: Rumskib Destroyer (Kitteh)
variants.description: The Republic Destroyer is the newest Navy design, a smaller version of the Cruiser designed to fight toe-to-toe with even the largest pirates. Although it can reach higher top speeds than any of the other large Navy ships, it takes longer to get there - unless it uses its afterburner.

variants.name: Sea Scorpion (Shredder, Jump)
variants.description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

variants.name: Marauder Leviathan (timeout)
variants.description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service for a long time, and captains have had some very interesting ideas about how to modify them for optimum performance. This one has had the hull surface completely stripped and replaced, with the new layout containing more shield projectors per square meter.

variants.name: Shield Beetle (Azure)
variants.description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

variants.name: Ibis (Zoura)
variants.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

variants.name: Heliarch Stalker (Zoura)
variants.description: Boasting incredible durability for a ship this size, the Stalker is a compact fortress that can take serious punishment and recover faster than some warships. Its speed and maneuverability, however, leave much to be desired. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Selii'mar (Zoura)
variants.description: The Selii'mar was a common Builder mining drone, one of the few automated ships they ever produced. The Het stations still produce them, in order to harvest the materials they need to keep working. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

variants.name: Cicada (Sanujab)
variants.description: A design nearly as old as the Aphid, practically no new Cicadas have been built in recent memory; it is so reliable that the second-hand market is saturated with Cicadas that have outlived not just one pilot, but in many cases whole lineages of Hai that have owned one and passed it down through the generations. This feature is likely the main factor which has kept the popularity of the Scarab in check as well as defining the Yeer e Ki shipyard's motto: "Service for Life."

variants.name: Scout (Intola)
variants.description: The Lionheart Scoutship was designed back in the days when the galaxy was more sparsely populated, and ships prospecting for habitable worlds or worlds rich in resources would often travel far away from any habitable ports where they would be able to refuel. Although the galaxy has now been much more thoroughly explored, the Scout remains a popular ship for long-distance travel.

variants.name: Splinter (Verdorve)
variants.description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by the Megaparsec shipyard. It is designed to be faster and more maneuverable than other ships of a similar size, in order to serve as a heavy escort ship or as part of a planetary defense and patrol fleet. Because of its intended use in merchant convoys, it also has a fair amount of cargo space.

variants.name: Shield Beetle (Sookoot)
variants.description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

variants.name: Clipper (Andy Lockman)
variants.description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

variants.name: Saber (Gatling-Built)
variants.description: During the caravan era, a boom of economic development spread along the paths of more heavily armed and technologically advanced merchant fleets originating from the Deep, which resulted in ships such as the Saber being developed. This medium warship was one of the powerhouses of military might in its time, armed to the teeth with five gun ports in the nose and a dorsal turret mount to fire at anything not in front of it. As pirates began to join together in larger war bands in response to the more heavily defended merchants, the Saber became popular amongst them as well. Unlike other ships such as the Firebird, it slowly faded out as competition rose, but it is nevertheless cherished by those who still own one.

variants.name: Sasumata (Angoma)
variants.description: The Sasumata is the chief scout ship of the Bunrodea. While not designed for combat, this stealth transport vessel is still capable of holding its own against some smaller warships; its four fighter bays turn the Sasumata into a force to be reckoned with when its fighters are deployed. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Sasumata's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Telis'het (Human Tech)
variants.description: When the Builders designed the exoskeleton of the Telis, they came to the conclusion that it would have been impossible to make it resilient enough without compromising its agility and integrity. Instead, they focused heavily on improving their shield technology, giving the Telis many of their regenerators.

variants.name: Modified Tachytia (WV-51)
variants.description: This was once an Avgi sublight starship, one of the last models built before the discovery of the hyperdrive. Most starships around this period used ram-augmented fusion drives to reach cruising speeds of up to 0.25c, but the Tachytia class was designed to reach a velocity of 0.4c with an antimatter fuel source to ignite propellant gathered with its huge ramscoop. 	This particular ship was never launched on its mission, commissioned just before the hyperdrive made it obsolete. Now, it has been extensively modified by removing many of its propellant tanks, replacing them with a hyperdrive, additional equipment spaces, and several docking bays. The cryosleep bay has also been substituted for spacious habitatation cylinders, making this a true city ship.

variants.name: Akoustiki (WV-51)
variants.description: The Akoustiki Excursion Vehicle is a modern take on the utility pod, a small personal craft designed to aid in the maintenance and repair of a station, or a carrier vessel and its escorts. It is just as commonly seen flitting about the orbitals as it is servicing satellites or acting as a personal transport shuttle between stations and low gravity bodies. Recent advances in magnetoplasmadynamic engines have made it a capable mining craft as well, albeit one with little cargo space for ores. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Waverider (WV-51)
variants.description: The Waverider was a ship that was first requested and never used for five years, seven months and twenty-seven days. 	Its disappointment was so immense, it willed itself into being.

variants.name: Vyrmeid (Hyperdrive)
variants.description: These are tiny space creatures that feed on solar energy, storing it into two receptors found at the front of their bodies. They are extremely docile, and are found in large groups, almost never seen alone.

outfits.name: Quad Beam Laser
outfits.description: In the early part of the space era, the settlements in the region of space known as the Deep developed in relative isolation from the rest of human space. One result of that isolation is that their weapons technology mostly uses beam weapons, instead of the energy projectile weapons that are more common elsewhere. 	The Quadruple Beam Laser is a modified version of the regular Beam Laser. The designers crammed together four beams for better space usage, but at the cost of less accuracy, more heat and less energy efficiency.

outfits.name: Quad Laser Turret
outfits.description: Unlike the Laser Turret, the quadified version does not turn fast enough to hold off fighter pilots. However, the doubled damage output more than makes up for this slight disadvantage. 	Although it would make more sense to mount a Quad Beam Laser on a turret, Deep Sky instead decided to double the lasers held by the normal laser turret as a cheaper way to increase the damage output.

outfits.name: Single Plasma Turret
outfits.description: The Single Plasma Turret is one plasma cannon mounted on a turret to fire in any direction. While not as powerful as the dual Plasma Turret, it is able to fit on much smaller ships.

outfits.name: Quad Plasma Turret
outfits.description: The Quad Plasma Turret is even more powerful than the standard Plasma Turret, but its size makes it rather impractical - it costs more than a flight of fighters and would occupy almost all of a medium warship's weapon space. As such, no sane independent captain will use it. 	So, how about one?

outfits.name: Rumskib Triple Gatling Gun
outfits.description: When Dr. Richard J. Gatling invented the rapid-fire gun bearing his name in the 19th century, he hoped that its destructiveness would lead to the end of war itself. Over a millennium later, this modern incarnation of the Gatling Gun sees action in the stars, a testament to its fundamentally robust design - and Dr. Gatling's naivete. 	This more powerful incarnation of the weapon fires three bullets simultaneously from a single gun port, at the cost of more energy for each individual shot. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: Rumskib Quad Gatling Turret
outfits.description: When the war broke out the Pirates had to adapt to the new circumstances. The Quad Gatling Turret was born when a pirate, taking a page from the Gatling Turret, thought to replace the blasters on a Quad Blaster Turret with a double pair of Gatling Guns. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: Rumskib Triple Gatling Turret
outfits.description: The standard version of the Gatling Turret in the Core. The Triple Gat Turret is an unnerving threat for any honest merchant, with more barrels then the default version. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: Rumskib Single Gatling Turret
outfits.description: In an act that would make Dr. Richard J. Gatling spin in his grave with the ferocity of the barrels of his invention, the Gatling Turret was born when a resourceful pirate thought to replace the Energy Blasters on a Blaster Turret with a pair of Gatling Guns. Ever since, the dominion of the Gatling Turret has spread across the Southern Rim, terrorizing merchants and law enforcement alike with its unparalleled ability to tear apart the hulls of ships. 	The Single Gatling Turret came into existence when a pirate decided that the space requirements for the Gatling Turret were too high and dismounted one of the Gatling guns, creating a turret that turns even faster. As a result, this turret is the best anti-fighter weapon employed by pirates. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.
